benchmark_annotation.summary: Enquiry about my account name

metrics.reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Dawn / Slurm Account / Project Charging
Technical Action Required: No (user guidance only)
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Medium (job submission blocked)

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User is trying to submit a Dawn GPU job and receives:

  sbatch: error: Batch job submission failed: Invalid account or account/partition combination specified

On Dawn, this error usually means that the `#SBATCH -A` line is using the wrong Slurm charging account or that the account/partition combination is invalid. The safe guidance is to tell the user to use their project charging account rather than their personal login name and to ask for the relevant `#SBATCH` lines if the account name is still unclear.

ACTION

1) Ask the user to send the exact `#SBATCH -A` and partition lines from the script if they are not already visible in the ticket.
2) Clarify the distinction:
   - Login username is not the same thing as the Slurm charging account.
   - `#SBATCH -A` must use the Dawn project/account code associated with the user's access.
3) If the user already knows their project account from the DAWN portal or onboarding information, tell them to use that exact project code in `#SBATCH -A`.

4) Do NOT:
   - Tell the user to put their username in `#SBATCH -A`.
   - Create a new project/account unless project assignment is actually wrong.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Hello Ruitian,

The submission error is most likely because Slurm expects the Dawn project charging account in the job script, not your personal login username.

Please check that your script uses the correct project account in the `#SBATCH -A` line. If you are not sure which project code to use, please send us the `#SBATCH -A` and partition lines from the script and we can check them.

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Keep login identity and Slurm charging account separate.
- Do not guess a replacement account if project assignment is unclear.
metrics.response:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: HPC / Slurm Submission
Technical Action Required: Yes
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The user is experiencing an error when submitting a Slurm script, specifically "sbatch: error: Batch job submission failed: Invalid account or account/partition combination specified" [1]. This error typically occurs when there is a mismatch between the account and partition specified in the Slurm script [2].

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
To resolve this issue, the user should verify that the account and partition specified in the Slurm script match the available resources on the HPC system [3]. The user can check their available accounts and partitions using the "mybalance" command [4]. If the account or partition is incorrect, the user should update the Slurm script accordingly.

QUESTIONS TO ASK (ONLY IF NEEDED)
- Can you please provide the output of the "mybalance" command to verify your available accounts and partitions?
- Have you checked the Slurm script for any typos or errors in the account and partition specifications?

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello,
Thank you for your help. I have checked my available accounts and partitions using the "mybalance" command, and I realized that I had a typo in my Slurm script. I have updated the script, and I will try submitting again.
Best regards,
Ruitian

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- It is essential to ensure that the account and partition specified in the Slurm script match the available resources on the HPC system to avoid errors and unauthorized access [5].
- Users should regularly check their available accounts and partitions using the "mybalance" command to ensure they are aware of any changes or updates [6].

REFERENCE KEY
[1] : HPCSSUP-57238
[2] : HPCSSUP-8291
[3] : HPCSSUP-15710
[4] : HPCSSUP-73623
[5] : HPCSSUP-10309
[6] : HPCSSUP-100255

with my account?
Thanks,
Julia
</MESSAGE>

<MESSAGE id=0003 role=HELPDESK_ASSIGNEE>
Dear Julia,
Actually you have an old project on Darwin called IIDA-SL3, and this has been mapped to a new project on the new CPU cluster, called IIDA-SL3-CPU.
Wilkes2 is the new gpu cluster (part of CSD3), and Wilkes1 is the old. 
Wilkes2 contains 4 P100 GPUs per node, Wilkes1 contains 2 K20c GPUs per node.
There are a few more details available here:
[https://www.csd3.cam.ac.uk/services/csd3-platform](https://www.csd3.cam.ac.uk/services/csd3-platform)
The Darwin login nodes (login.hpc) are only accessible from inside the Cambridge network. If you are outside this, you can still ssh into the CSD3 login nodes (login-cpu.hpc and login-gpu.hpc).
Best regards
Stuart
</MESSAGE>

<MESSAGE id=0004 role=TICKET_CREATOR>
Dear Stuart,
I see what you mean. Could you create a project for me on wilkes2? I 
would like to do some machine learning training on the GPU cluster. Is 
there any other information I need to provide?
Thanks,
Julia
</MESSAGE>

<MESSAGE id=0005 role=HELPDESK_ASSIGNEE>
Dear Julia,
I have created a new IIDA-SL3-GPU project for you on Wilkes2.
Best regards,
Stuart
</MESSAGE>

benchmark_annotation.summary: Query on extending SL3 CPU usage and annual reset

metrics.reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: SL3 / CPU Credits / Service Level Policy
Technical Action Required: No
Finance Action Required: No
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Low

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User reports reaching SL3 CPU allocation and asks:
1) Whether additional CPU hours can be requested.
2) Whether SL3 free usage resets annually and when.

SL3 CPU credits are allocated at the project account level (shared across all users on that project). Credits are refreshed quarterly (not annually) at the start of each academic quarter.

ACTION

1) Clarify allocation model
   - SL3 CPU credits are assigned to the project account.
   - Usage is shared across all members of that project.
   - Individual users do not receive personal allocations.

2) Clarify refresh schedule
   - Credits refresh at the start of each academic quarter:
     • 1 February – 30 April
     • 1 May – 31 July
     • 1 August – 31 October
     • 1 November – 31 January

3) Direct to policy documentation
   - Service levels and SL3 policy page.

4) Do not:
   - Manually add credits.
   - Promise additional allocation.
   - Escalate unless there is a policy exception.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Subject: Re: SL3 CPU allocation and reset

Hello Wenhao,

SL3 CPU credits are allocated to the project account and are shared between all users who have access to that account — they are not assigned per individual user.

SL3 project credits are refreshed at the start of each academic quarter (three-month periods):
- 1 February – 30 April
- 1 May – 31 July
- 1 August – 31 October
- 1 November – 31 January

You can read more about SL3 service levels and policies here:
https://docs.hpc.cam.ac.uk/hpc/user-guide/policies.html#service-levels

Please let us know if you have any further questions.

Best regards,  
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Do not imply additional CPU hours can be granted outside policy.
- Do not treat SL3 credits as personal allocations.
- Do not promise resets outside the defined quarterly schedule.

According to the documentation, each PI may receive 200,000 CPU core hours and 3000 GPU hours per quarter for SL3 [2]. The user's current usage on account wx266 has reached the limit, with 380,694 CPU hours used under Account SMIELEWSKI-SL3-CPU over the last year [1].

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
1. Verify the user's current usage and allocation [1].
2. Check the documentation for SL3 usage and reset policies [2].
3. Inform the user that SL3 usage is reset every quarter, with 200,000 CPU core hours and 3000 GPU hours available per quarter [2].
4. Advise the user to consult with their PI or research group to purchase additional CPU hours if needed [1][3].

QUESTIONS TO ASK (ONLY IF NEEDED)
- Can you provide more information about your research group's funding and allocation for HPC resources?
- Have you discussed your usage and allocation with your PI or research group?

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Thank you for your inquiry about extending your SL3 CPU allocation. According to our documentation, each PI may receive 200,000 CPU core hours and 3000 GPU hours per quarter for SL3 [2]. Your current usage on account wx266 has reached the limit, with 380,694 CPU hours used under Account SMIELEWSKI-SL3-CPU over the last year [1]. If you need additional CPU hours, please consult with your PI or research group to purchase more hours [1][3].

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- SL3 usage is reset every quarter, with 200,000 CPU core hours and 3000 GPU hours available per quarter [2].
- Users should consult with their PI or research group to purchase additional CPU hours if needed [1][3].

Dear HPC Group,
I am Wenhao Xu, a PhD student in the Department of Clinical Neurosciences, working in Prof. Peter Smielewski’s group. I am writing to ask about extending our SL3 CPU allocation, as my current usage on account wx266 has reached the limit.
According to mybalance records, I have used 380,694 CPU hours under Account SMIELEWSKI-SL3-CPU over the last year, and I would be grateful if you could advise:

Whether it is possible to request additional CPU hours for continued work this year; and
1.
Whether SL3 free usage reset annually, and if so, when the reset could occur.
If you need any additional details, please let me know.
Many thanks for your help.
Best,
–
Wenhao Xu
PhD candidate, Department of Clinical Neurosciences
Clare Hall, University of Cambridge

Hi Pok-Man,
And a very happy new year to you too.
I’m a bit puzzled with this bit:
“we would like to find a way to let my HPC account accessing Marc's lab group HPC quota. Currently Marc's lab group has not sign up with the HPC, the same situation as Martin's when I started my years here in 2021.”
If Marc is not signed up to HPC, what quota are you then talking about?
Because I am very fearful that both Martin and Marc are running with old facilities in mind. So, up until 2022, they could get free processing and storage on the HPC (within limits) because the School was paying the bill for them. The ideal of the School scheme was to give our Departments the change to experience HPC and see if it works well, and delivers on its promises with how fast you can process on it etc.
But that scheme had ended. Now if anyone wants to process on the HPC (and they have both memory processing and CPU processing) then you need to buy hours on the HPC to do that.
So, If Marc does not have any CPU time on HPC for you to use, then this request does not make sense to me. IT would mean that you/Martin would need to buy your own time on the HPC.
I suggest you contact purchases@hpc.cam.ac.uk<[purchases@hpc.cam.ac.uk](mailto:purchases@hpc.cam.ac.uk)> to discuss how much processing hours on CPU you think you would need so they can quote that for you. And your welcome to ask them if any free hours are available.
Regards,
Matthijs

[STATUS] RESOLVED
[CREATED] 2021-10-29T10:53:52.851+0100
[SUMMARY] Computing services

[INITIAL_DESCRIPTION]
Hello,  
I am writing to ask for your assistance with choosing a computing service for MATLAB jobs. I have a high number of MATLAB jobs (between 500 - 1000), and depending on the MATLAB script, a job can take several hours (in some cases > 5 hours) on a decent laptop. The scripts are not written in a way that the computations can be performed in parallel (other than MATLAB's internal functions). In my previous organisations, I used computation clusters (e.g., SGE) to submit many MATLAB jobs at a time. Is there such a computing service available at the University of Cambridge? I would be grateful if you could advise which computing service would most suit MATLAB jobs of this size.  
Many thanks for your help.  
Kind regards, 
Berk  
Muammer Berk MirzaResearch Associate Prediction and Learning Lab Department of Psychology Cambridge University

[CONVERSATION]
<MESSAGE id=0001 role=HELPDESK_ASSIGNEE>
Dear Berk,
The 'CSD3' HPC cluster would be suitable for your needs I think. Matlab is used quite extensively to process data using it, I'd suggest applying for an account via the form at [https://www.hpc.cam.ac.uk/rcs-application](https://www.hpc.cam.ac.uk/rcs-application) giving the name of your PI, if you aren't one yourself.
This is a brief page from our user documentation on using Matlab on the cluster, once you have an account - [https://docs.hpc.cam.ac.uk/hpc/software-packages/matlab.html](https://docs.hpc.cam.ac.uk/hpc/software-packages/matlab.html)
We have versions between r2015b and 2021b available of Matlab currently.
There is a 'quick start' guide on using the cluster at [https://docs.hpc.cam.ac.uk/hpc/user-guide/quickstart.html](https://docs.hpc.cam.ac.uk/hpc/user-guide/quickstart.html) but you can also ask any general questions via a ticket to [support@hpc.cam.ac.uk](mailto:support@hpc.cam.ac.uk) as well.
Regards
Leon
Research Computing Services
</MESSAGE>

<MESSAGE id=0002 role=HELPDESK_ASSIGNEE>

benchmark_annotation.summary: Interactive Jupyter Notebooks crashing before loading

metrics.reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Open OnDemand / Jupyter / Service Disruption
Technical Action Required: Possibly (service-side investigation)
Escalation Required: Yes (if filesystem/service incident ongoing)
Urgency: Medium (interactive workflows blocked)

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User reports that OnDemand Jupyter notebooks reach “Starting” state and then crash. The output log shows:

- `module: command not found`
- `jupyter: command not found`
- Timeout waiting for notebook port

These errors normally indicate that:
- The module environment was not initialised properly, or
- The underlying filesystem/environment (e.g. RDS) was unavailable at session start.

Because multiple core commands are missing inside an OnDemand-generated script, helpdesk should first check for an active service incident before asking the user to debug their own notebook configuration.

ACTION (HELPDESK)

1) Check for active incidents
   - Verify whether RDS or related shared filesystems are degraded.
   - Confirm whether other users are reporting similar failures.
   - Check service status / internal comms.

2) Do NOT ask the user to debug their environment immediately
   - The presence of multiple `module: command not found` entries in an OnDemand-generated script strongly suggests an environment failure rather than a user error.
   - Avoid requesting script changes unless no incident exists.

3) If a disruption is confirmed
   - Inform the user that a service issue is being investigated.
   - Provide reassurance that notebooks should function again once the service issue is cleared.

4) If no incident is active (alternate branch)
   - Check:
       echo $MODULEPATH
       which module
       module list
       which jupyter
   - Confirm correct partition/environment in OnDemand form.
   - Escalate to platform admins if environment initialisation is broken.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Subject: Re: Interactive Jupyter Notebooks crashing

Hello Tim,

The log suggests that the OnDemand environment failed to initialise correctly rather than that there is a notebook configuration error on your side.

We are checking whether there is a current service issue affecting the shared environment or filesystem. We will update you as soon as we have confirmed that.

Best wishes,  
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Do not attribute failure to user configuration when a known service disruption exists.
- Do not suggest modifying system scripts under /ondemand.
- Avoid unnecessary troubleshooting steps during confirmed incidents.
- If repeated reports occur without an active incident, escalate to platform administrators.
metrics.response:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Jupyter Notebook / OnDemand [1][2]
Technical Action Required: Yes [1][2]
Portal Action Required: Yes (OnDemand) [1][2]
Urgency: Medium [1][2]

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The user is experiencing issues with their interactive OnDemand notebooks, which seem to reach the point of "Starting" and then crash before they can actually begin [1][2]. The provided output.log file indicates that the Jupyter Notebook server is unable to open the specified port, and there are errors related to the "module" command not being found [1][2]. This suggests a potential issue with the environment or module configuration [1][2].

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
1. Request the user to check their environment and module configuration to ensure that the necessary modules are loaded [1][2].
2. Ask the user to try setting up a new interactive session with the correct partition and modules, such as "desktop" under Partition and "jupyterlab/3" under Modules [2].
3. If the issue persists, request the user to provide more information about their environment, such as the output of `module list` and `conda info` [1][2].

QUESTIONS TO ASK (ONLY IF NEEDED)
- Can you please provide a screenshot of your interactive session configuration page [2]?
- Have you tried running your Jupyter notebook on a compute node instead of a login node [1][2]?

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello,
Thank you for your assistance. I have checked my environment and module configuration, and I will try setting up a new interactive session with the correct partition and modules. If the issue persists, I will provide more information about my environment.
Best regards,
Tim

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Ensure that the user is using the correct partition and modules for their Jupyter notebook session [1][2].
- Be cautious when troubleshooting issues related to environment and module configuration to avoid potential conflicts or errors [1][2].

REFERENCE KEY
[1] : HPCSSUP-65771
[2] : HPCSSUP-99376

Dear Ayden,
Can you provide some more information about the Jupyter notebook error on login-web. Can you send me a screenshot of the new Jupyter Notebook session page so I can verify that you’ve used the correct modules and partition. After it fails to open the notebook can you click on the Session ID and find the output.log file? If so can you please send me the file so I can investigate the cause of this issue.
For your second question in which node are you running the notebook? What do you mean the SLURM resources show as allocated? In order you run the notebook on a compute node you first get a compute or gpu node with srun or sintr from a SSH session then you start Jupyter on the compute/gpu node as usual and finally you port forward through a new SSH connection from your local machine to the compute/gpu node the notebook is running. See our instructions here: https://docs.hpc.cam.ac.uk/hpc/software-packages/jupyter.html#running-jupyter-on-a-compute-node  . Once all that is done you can connect to the notebook from your local browser and any scripts will run on the remote compute/gpu node, you don’t need to run “srun” or any other slurm commands from within the notebook.
Kind regards,
Avraam

benchmark_annotation.summary: Compiling LAMMPS on Ampere

metrics.reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Software / Compilation / GPU (Ampere)
Technical Action Required: No (user to retry with updated stack)
Escalation Required: Possibly (if issue persists after stack update)
Urgency: Low–Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User reports CUDA driver errors when compiling a newer version of LAMMPS with the GPU package on Ampere. The older stable version works.

Likely cause: mismatch between CUDA/toolchain versions and the currently loaded software stack.

ACTION

Ask the user to retry compilation using the updated module stack:

    module load rhel8/ampere/base
    module load gcc/14.3.0/vlhhcp6m
    module load cmake/3.31.10/gcc/7ddsybx7
    module load cuda/12.8.1/gcc/kdeps6ab
    module load openmpi/4.1.8/gcc/hemliivg

Then re-run the build and benchmark test.

If the issue persists after loading the updated stack, request:
- Full build output
- Exact LAMMPS version
- Full error message from runtime test

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Hi Max,

Could you please try compiling again using the newest Ampere software stack:

  module load rhel8/ampere/base
  module load gcc/14.3.0/vlhhcp6m
  module load cmake/3.31.10/gcc/7ddsybx7
  module load cuda/12.8.1/gcc/kdeps6ab
  module load openmpi/4.1.8/gcc/hemliivg

After loading these modules, please rebuild and test again.

Let us know how you get on.

Best wishes,
HPC Support


SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Do not recommend installing custom CUDA drivers.
- Do not suggest modifying system-level GPU drivers.
- Keep troubleshooting limited to supported module stacks unless escalation is required.

[TICKET_SUMMARY] Running LAMMPS on a single GPU

[CONVERSATION]
<MESSAGE id=0001 role=HELPDESK_ASSIGNEE>
Hi Emma, 
As our docs page outlines: 
> …making use of all 4 GPUs on 1 compute node, running 2 MPI tasks per GPU thanks to the [CUDA Multi-Process Service (MPS)](https://docs.nvidia.com/deploy/mps/index.html) wrapper script detailed inside. This wrapper script allows to run efficiently and concurrently multiple MPI processes on a GPU by making use of the Hyper-Q capabilities on the latest NVIDIA cards. This allowing to optimise the GPU usage when each MPI process only use a fraction of the GPU memory available (80 GB on the Ampere cards). Without the MPS, the MPI processes will be run sequentially, wasting GPU time. 
Can you show me the error messages from this single GPU run? Ideally an err/out file. Thanks 
Best wishes, 
Henry

Hi Yin,

#download LAMMPS
git clone --depth=1 
[https://github.com/lammps/lammps.git](https://github.com/lammps/lammps.git) lammps-gpu
cd lammps-gpu

module purge
module load rhel7/default-gpu
#build the gpu support library with P100 support
pushd lib/gpu
export CUDA_HOME=/usr/local/Cluster-Apps/cuda/8.0
sed -i 's/CUDA_ARCH =.*/CUDA_ARCH = -arch=sm_60/' Makefile.linux
make -j -f Makefile.linux
popd
#load modules for the main make
module load gcc/9
module load openmpi/gcc/9.3/4.0.4
pushd src
#activate support for EAM forcefields
make yes-manybody
#activate support for GPUs
make yes-gpu
#build lammps with mpi support
make -j mpi
popd

Dominic

[CONVERSATION]
<MESSAGE id=0001 role=HELPDESK_ASSIGNEE>
Hi Emma, 
As our docs page outlines: 
> …making use of all 4 GPUs on 1 compute node, running 2 MPI tasks per GPU thanks to the [CUDA Multi-Process Service (MPS)](https://docs.nvidia.com/deploy/mps/index.html) wrapper script detailed inside. This wrapper script allows to run efficiently and concurrently multiple MPI processes on a GPU by making use of the Hyper-Q capabilities on the latest NVIDIA cards. This allowing to optimise the GPU usage when each MPI process only use a fraction of the GPU memory available (80 GB on the Ampere cards). Without the MPS, the MPI processes will be run sequentially, wasting GPU time. 
Can you show me the error messages from this single GPU run? Ideally an err/out file. Thanks 
Best wishes, 
Henry
HPC Support

Dear Andrés,
The latest gcc version for the login-e nodes (that you are using) is gcc/9. For the login-icelake nodes that are using a later version of CentOS the latest version of gcc is gcc/11 and gcc/11.2.0/gcc/rjvgspag. If you see any modules with gcc in the name, that is the version of gcc used to build the software. It doesn’t mean it’s the latest gcc version or that you should use the same gcc version to build any other software you need. You can find the available versions of a module with the command: “module avail 2>&1 | grep <module_name>”. Please try one of the latest version of gcc and let me know if you get any other errors.
For your reference the latest version of LAMMPS available on our cluster is lammps/23Jun2022.
Kind regards,
Avraam

benchmark_annotation.summary: Fw: DAWN Access Assistance

metrics.reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Account Provisioning / DAWN / Project Access
Technical Action Required: Yes (account provisioning once invitation accepted)
Escalation Required: Possibly (confirm invitation + provisioning state)
Urgency: Medium (user awaiting access)

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User is requesting access to DAWN and has provided name, email, and SSH key.

Correct process for DAWN access:
1) User must be invited to the DAWN project by the PI via Waldur.
2) User must accept the invitation in the DAWN access portal.
3) Once accepted, provisioning can be checked and the local DAWN account can then be created through the approved workflow.

Helpdesk should NOT:
- Manually create accounts before invitation is accepted.
- Install SSH keys manually from email.
- Bypass the Waldur provisioning workflow.

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)

1) Confirm invitation status
   - Check whether the PI has invited the user in Waldur.
   - If not invited → instruct user to contact PI.

2) Confirm invitation acceptance
   - If invited but not accepted → instruct user to log into portal and accept invitation.
   - Provide documentation link.

3) If the user says they have already accepted the invitation
   - Check whether provisioning has started or whether a provisioning ticket exists.
   - Escalate internally if the invitation has been accepted but no account-provisioning progress is visible.

4) Do NOT:
   - Accept SSH keys via email.
   - Manually append SSH keys.
   - Share temporary credentials.
   - Create accounts outside the provisioning system.

IF USER PROVIDES SSH KEY
- Acknowledge receipt but do not install manually.
- Once account exists, user can install key themselves after first login.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Hello Jun,

You need to be invited to the DAWN project by the PI via Waldur. Once you have received the invitation, please accept it in the DAWN access portal before we can proceed with checking your login-account provisioning.

Please see this guidance:
https://docs.hpc.cam.ac.uk/hpc/user-guide/pvc.html#users-accessing-open-on-demand-via-the-portal

Best wishes,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Never manually install SSH keys received via email.
- Never request or handle private keys.
- Do not create accounts outside the approved provisioning workflow.
- Ensure invitation acceptance precedes account creation.
- Do not bypass Waldur/project-based access controls.

reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Account Provisioning / DAWN / Project Access
Technical Action Required: Yes (account provisioning once invitation accepted)
Escalation Required: Possibly (confirm invitation + provisioning state)
Urgency: Medium (user awaiting access)

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User is requesting access to DAWN and has provided name, email, and SSH key.

Correct process for DAWN access:
1) User must be invited to the DAWN project by the PI via Waldur.
2) User must accept the invitation in the DAWN access portal.
3) Once accepted, provisioning can be checked and the local DAWN account can then be created through the approved workflow.

Helpdesk should NOT:
- Manually create accounts before invitation is accepted.
- Install SSH keys manually from email.
- Bypass the Waldur provisioning workflow.

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)

1) Confirm invitation status
   - Check whether the PI has invited the user in Waldur.
   - If not invited → instruct user to contact PI.

2) Confirm invitation acceptance
   - If invited but not accepted → instruct user to log into portal and accept invitation.
   - Provide documentation link.

3) If the user says they have already accepted the invitation
   - Check whether provisioning has started or whether a provisioning ticket exists.
   - Escalate internally if the invitation has been accepted but no account-provisioning progress is visible.

4) Do NOT:
   - Accept SSH keys via email.
   - Manually append SSH keys.
   - Share temporary credentials.
   - Create accounts outside the provisioning system.

IF USER PROVIDES SSH KEY
- Acknowledge receipt but do not install manually.
- Once account exists, user can install key themselves after first login.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Hello Jun,

You need to be invited to the DAWN project by the PI via Waldur. Once you have received the invitation, please accept it in the DAWN access portal before we can proceed with checking your login-account provisioning.

Please see this guidance:
https://docs.hpc.cam.ac.uk/hpc/user-guide/pvc.html#users-accessing-open-on-demand-via-the-portal

Best wishes,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Never manually install SSH keys received via email.
- Never request or handle private keys.
- Do not create accounts outside the approved provisioning workflow.
- Ensure invitation acceptance precedes account creation.
- Do not bypass Waldur/project-based access controls.

benchmark_annotation.summary: Cannot login to the HPC web portal

metrics.reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Authentication / Web Portal / Browser Behaviour
Technical Action Required: No (user-side troubleshooting)
Escalation Required: No (unless issue persists across users)
Urgency: Medium (access blocked)

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User reports that they still cannot log in via:

  https://login-web.hpc.cam.ac.uk/

despite being told the login issue was resolved.

This is most consistent with:
- Browser caching/session persistence issues, or
- A stale redirect/session cookie.

No evidence of account-level authentication failure.

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)

1) Rule out browser cache/session issue
   Ask user to:
   - Clear browser cache and cookies for login-web.hpc.cam.ac.uk
   - Try an incognito/private window
   - Try a different browser

2) Provide forced redirect link
   Ask them to test:

   https://login-web.hpc.cam.ac.uk/nginx/stop?redir=/pun/sys/dashboard/

   This forces the nginx redirect to reset the login path.

3) Confirm behaviour
   - If redirect link works → likely cached session issue.
   - If both links fail → escalate to web portal admins.

4) Do NOT:
   - Reset password unless authentication failure is confirmed.
   - Modify account without evidence.
   - Assume global outage unless multiple reports exist.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Hello Yiran,

Could you please try clearing your browser cache or opening the portal in a private/incognito window?

If that does not resolve it, please try logging in via the following link:

  https://login-web.hpc.cam.ac.uk/nginx/stop?redir=/pun/sys/dashboard/

This forces a redirect and can resolve cached session issues.

Please let us know whether this works.

Best wishes,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Do not imply account compromise.
- Do not suggest credential changes unless authentication failure is confirmed.
- Escalate only if issue persists across multiple users or browsers.
metrics.response:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Login Issue [1][3]
Technical Action Required: Possibly [5]
Urgency: Medium [3]

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The user is experiencing issues logging in to the HPC web portal [https://login-web.hpc.cam.ac.uk/](https://login-web.hpc.cam.ac.uk/), despite receiving an email stating that the issue has been resolved [3]. Previous tickets suggest that login issues can be caused by various factors, including incorrect credentials [1], account locks due to multiple failed login attempts [6], and issues with two-factor authentication [7].

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
1. Request the user to confirm their login credentials, including their full Cambridge email address and Raven password [1].
2. Ask the user to check if they have been locked out due to multiple failed login attempts and if they have tried waiting for 20 minutes before attempting to log in again [1].
3. If the user is still experiencing issues, request them to provide more details about the error message they are seeing, if any [3].

QUESTIONS TO ASK (ONLY IF NEEDED)
- Can you please provide a screenshot of the error message you are seeing when trying to log in?
- Have you tried logging in using a different browser or device?
- Are you experiencing any issues with your two-factor authentication?

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello,
Thank you for reaching out. I have tried logging in again with my full Cambridge email address and Raven password, but I am still experiencing issues. I have also waited for 20 minutes after multiple failed login attempts. I am seeing an error message that says "Invalid username or password". I have tried logging in using a different browser, but the issue persists.
Best regards,
Yiran

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- It is essential to ensure that the user's account is not locked due to multiple failed login attempts, as this can cause unnecessary delays [6].
- The user should be advised to use their full Cambridge email address and Raven password when logging in to the HPC web portal [1].

REFERENCE KEY
[1] : HPCSSUP-86199
[3] : HPCSSUP-86335
[5] : HPCSSUP-86374
[6] : HPCSSUP-75785
[7] : HPCSSUP-81661

benchmark_annotation.summary: Scheduling jobs during maintenance

metrics.reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Slurm / Cluster Performance / Power Management
Technical Action Required: No (policy clarification only)
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Low–Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User observes that jobs submitted during reduced power consumption periods appear to run significantly slower and may time out. They ask whether there is a Slurm constraint (e.g. a #SBATCH flag) to prevent jobs from starting when the cluster is in a low-power state.

On CSD3, Slurm does not start jobs on “degraded performance” nodes. Jobs are scheduled only when requested resources are available. Reduced power states do not cause Slurm to allocate partially performing resources. 

Apparent slowdowns are more likely caused by:
- I/O contention (e.g. high demand on RDS shared filesystem),
- General cluster load,
- Application-level variability.

There is no Slurm flag to prevent job start based on power mode, as power state does not change the allocated CPU/GPU specification.

ACTION

1) Clarify scheduler behaviour
   - Slurm will only start jobs once requested resources are available.
   - Reduced power consumption periods do not change the allocated hardware specification for a job.

2) Explain likely causes of slowdown
   - Shared filesystem (RDS) performance varies depending on demand.
   - High I/O workloads across users can impact runtime.
   - Jobs may therefore run longer than expected.

3) Provide practical mitigation advice
   - Ensure jobs request realistic walltime.
   - Consider profiling I/O behaviour.
   - If appropriate, prevent automatic requeue by adding:
       #SBATCH --no-requeue
     (only if user specifically wants jobs not to restart after interruption).

4) Do NOT:
   - Suggest that jobs are intentionally throttled.
   - Promise guaranteed full-speed operation during all cluster states.
   - Suggest non-existent Slurm flags related to power state.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Subject: Re: Job behaviour during reduced power periods

Hi Clara,

The cluster power status does not affect how Slurm allocates resources. Slurm will only start your job once the requested CPUs/GPUs become available, and jobs are not started on partially performing hardware.

If you are seeing slowdowns, this is more likely due to general system load or performance variation on the shared RDS filesystem, whose performance can fluctuate depending on demand.

There isn’t a Slurm flag that prevents jobs from starting based on power state. If needed, you can ensure your jobs are not requeued automatically by adding:

  #SBATCH --no-requeue

Otherwise, we would recommend reviewing walltime requests and considering whether I/O demand could be contributing to the longer runtimes.

Best wishes,  
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Do not imply cluster misconfiguration without evidence.
- Do not suggest unsupported scheduler constraints.
- Avoid attributing performance issues to “power throttling” unless formally confirmed by cluster administrators.

Technical Action Required: Yes [1]
Portal Action Required: No [1]

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The user is experiencing issues with jobs running at a significantly lower speed during reduced power consumption periods, resulting in timeouts and loss of compute hours [1]. The user is looking for a way to prevent jobs from starting if the cluster is in a low-power state [1].

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
To address the user's concern, we need to investigate the current power restrictions and their impact on job scheduling [8]. We should also review the user's job submission script to ensure it is optimized for the current cluster configuration [4].

QUESTIONS TO ASK (ONLY IF NEEDED)
- Can you provide more information about the jobs that are timing out, such as the job IDs and submission scripts [1]?
- Have you noticed any patterns or correlations between the low-power periods and the job timeouts [1]?

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello,
Thank you for reaching out to us about your concerns with job timeouts during reduced power consumption periods. We are currently investigating the issue and will provide an update soon. In the meantime, please let us know if you have any further questions or concerns.
Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- The current power restrictions may impact job scheduling and performance [8].
- Users should optimize their job submission scripts to minimize waste of compute resources [4].

[4] : HPCSSUP-29372
[8] : HPCSSUP-89989
metrics.user_input:
Good morning,
I have a question regarding the cluster’s behavior during reduced power consumption periods. I’ve noticed that jobs submitted during these windows are accepted and scheduled, but they execute at a significantly lower speed. This has led to several jobs timing out before completion, resulting in a loss of compute hours and requiring expensive reruns (even when requesting the same amount of resources as previously successful jobs).
Is there a way to prevent jobs from starting if the cluster is in a low-power state? Specifically, I’m looking for a requirement or constraint I can add to my job scripts (e.g., a specific #SLURM flag) to ensure they only run when full performance resources are available.
Thank you for your help!
Best regards,
Clara

Thank you for your help!
Best regards,
Clara

retrieval_trace.text:
[TICKET_SUMMARY] SLURM: Concerning Queue Time

[INITIAL_DESCRIPTION]
Hello,
My name is Jacob, and I occasionally submit SLURM jobs to the Icelake CPU cluster.
Roughly 5 days ago, I submitted ~20 jobs to Icelake, each requesting 1 node, 16 CPUs, and a maximum time limit of 12 hours.  It's been 5 days, and my jobs still haven't run; they're sitting indefinitely in the queue:
[cid:d3e486ac-2b91-4104-bf38-8d7f36d648ab]
I've submitted similar jobs before, and I never had jobs stuck in the queue this long. These particular jobs are for an upcoming, time-sensitive deadline.
Thus, I wanted to ask you:
1.
Is there any way you can prioritise these jobs so they're run and I can meet my deadline?
2.
Is there any way I can estimate queue times before I submit future jobs?
I apologise for sending this enquiry, as I know this long queue time was unintentional. However, I hope there's something you can do to allow these highly time-sensitive jobs to be run...
Jacob

[TICKET_SUMMARY] Job submission

[CONVERSATION]
<MESSAGE id=0001 role=HELPDESK_ASSIGNEE>
Dear Steve,
Can you share a job submission script so I can have a look at the resources you are requesting. During the cluster maintenance in July we decommissioned a large part of the cluster which might have caused longer queues times for the remaining partitions. We are in the process of adding more compute capacity in September and October which will create a new partition and hopefully reduce the wait times for jobs in the queue.
Kind regards,
Avraam

</MESSAGE><MESSAGE id=0002 role=TICKET_CREATOR>
Hi,
I ran someone else’s batch file and it ran instantly.
I noticed I had written the following in my batch script, I removed it and it seems to be working a lot better today. I am wondering if this requests a specific node when added to a script like this.

#SBATCH -c 1
Thanks,
Steve

<MESSAGE id=0005 role=HELPDESK_ASSIGNEE>
Hi Chen, 
No if the job completes then it won’t hang on the compute nodes. It’s generally best practice to request a wallclock time commensurate  with the amount of time needed for the job to complete. This helps get the job running quicker and reduces wasted compute time (which in the case of SL2 accounts will also conserve valuable resources). Thanks. 
Best wishes, 
Henry

[CONVERSATION]
<MESSAGE id=0001 role=HELPDESK_ASSIGNEE>
Hi Pier,
The cluster is highly subscribed at the moment and queuing times, particularly for jobs submitted under SL3 accounts, are very long.
Your request 12 hours on a single node is the maximum time which we allow SL3 users to run for. To improve your queue times it is recommended that you only request the time you need. I.e. if your code only runs for 2 hours, then requesting 12 hours is a waste of 10 hours, and has only made your job harder to schedule, a better request would be for 2.5 hours (to allow for run-time differences). 
Similarly, tuning your request for cores, nodes and perhaps utilising the array job function (if appropriate) can shorten queue wait times.
Obviously if you need a single node for 12 hours then there is little which can be done.
I hope this info helps, if you have any further questions, please let me know.
Dominic
</MESSAGE>

<MESSAGE id=0002 role=TICKET_CREATOR>

the same time.

benchmark_annotation.summary: Unusually long queue time

metrics.reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Slurm / Scheduling / Queue Wait Time
Technical Action Required: No (user guidance only)
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Low

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User reports that a short GPU job has been waiting much longer than usual. This does not by itself indicate an account or script fault; queue time can increase when demand is high, and queued jobs gain priority over time.

Cancelling and resubmitting is counterproductive because it removes the job's accumulated queue priority.

ACTION

1) Reassure the user that long waits can happen when demand is high, even for jobs that usually start quickly.
2) Advise the user not to cancel and resubmit queued jobs unless necessary.
3) If they want a rough estimate, suggest:
   `squeue -u <CRSid> --start`
4) Clarify that starting an interactive job does not change the priority of an already queued batch job.
5) Escalate only if there is evidence of scheduler malfunction rather than normal contention.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Dear Kai,

From the information provided, there does not appear to be anything obviously wrong with your job submission. Queue times can change significantly depending on current demand, so a job that usually starts quickly may occasionally wait much longer.

Please avoid cancelling and resubmitting the job unless necessary, as that resets its place in the queue and removes any priority it has accumulated while waiting.

If you want a rough estimate of when a queued job may start, you can run:

  squeue -u <your CRSid> --start

Also, starting an interactive job will not alter the priority of an existing queued batch job.

Best wishes,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Do not promise a fixed start time.
- Do not imply scheduler fault without evidence.
- Warn users that cancelling/resubmitting resets queue age.

metrics.reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Authentication / SSH Login / Password Clarification
Technical Action Required: No (user guidance only)
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Medium (access blocked)

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User is attempting to SSH to login-icelake.hpc.cam.ac.uk and reports not knowing their HPC password, stating they may never have set one.

On CSD3, there is no separate HPC-specific password for internal users. Authentication uses the user’s UIS/Raven password, followed by the appropriate CSD3 TOTP token for SSH access.

ACTION

1) Clarify authentication model
   - HPC password = UIS/Raven password.
   - There is no separate password to set during activation.

2) Clarify SSH login sequence
   When connecting via:
     ssh <CRSid>@login-icelake.hpc.cam.ac.uk

   The user should:
     a) Enter their current UIS/Raven password.
     b) Enter their TOTP code associated with CSD3 SSH (typically labelled “CSD3:ssh” or similar in their authenticator app).

3) If login still fails
   Ask:
     - Have you recently changed your UIS/Raven password?
     - Do you have the correct CSD3 SSH MFA token configured?
     - What exact error message appears after entering password/TOTP?

4) Do NOT:
   - Reset password directly.
   - Provide or request passwords.
   - Create a separate HPC password.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Subject: Re: HPC password

Dear Fay,

There is no separate HPC password to set up. Your HPC login password is the same as your UIS/Raven password.

When connecting via SSH, please enter your current UIS/Raven password when prompted, followed by the TOTP code associated with your CSD3 SSH login (usually labelled “CSD3:ssh” in your authenticator app).

If you continue to experience issues, please let us know the exact error message shown after entering your credentials.

Best wishes,  
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Never request or store user passwords.
- Do not imply a separate HPC password exists.
- Escalate only if authentication failures persist after confirming correct credential usage.

CLASSIFICATION
Category: Account / Login Issue
Technical Action Required: Yes
Portal Action Required: No
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The user is having trouble logging in to their HPC account and does not know their password [1]. The user has tried using their Raven password, but it does not work [2]. 

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
To resolve this issue, we need to determine if the user has a password set for their HPC account. If not, we can set a password for them. Alternatively, if the user has an SSH key, we can use that for authentication [3]. 

QUESTIONS TO ASK (ONLY IF NEEDED)
- Have you tried using your Raven password to log in to your HPC account?
- Do you have an SSH key that you can use for authentication?

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello,
Thank you for your help. I have tried using my Raven password, but it does not work. I do not have an SSH key, can you help me set one up?
Best regards,
Fay

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- We cannot provide passwords by email [4].
- It is recommended to use SSH key authentication instead of passwords [5].

REFERENCE KEY
[1] : HPCSSUP-41367
[2] : HPCSSUP-68215
[3] : HPCSSUP-43190
[4] : HPCSSUP-70553
[5] : HPCSSUP-41372

[TICKET_SUMMARY] Re: New Cambridge HPCS Account (hpckoh1)

[INITIAL_DESCRIPTION]

﻿On 12/06/2017, 12:56, "support@hpc.cam.ac.uk" <support@hpc.cam.ac.uk> wrote:
Your account on the HPCS has now been set up and the name of your login
account is hpckoh1. Please either ring us to collect a password on
(+ 44) (0)1223 763517
or email to this address a SSH public key (RSA, 2048 bits minimum) to use for
authentication.
Please don't hesitate to ask us directly by email to support@hpc.cam.ac.uk
or by phone if any of the following details need explanation or clarification.
Logging into the HPCS
—
Firstly, please ensure that any self-administered machines (e.g. laptops)
which you use to login to the HPCS are fully up to date with respect to 
operating system updates, antivirus and anti-spyware software.
We will need to explicitly allow external IP addresses access through our
firewall. Please email us the appropriate originating IP address for your
local network if you have not already done so.
You should be able to login to one of the login nodes of the cluster
simply by doing:
ssh hpckoh1@login.hpc.cam.ac.uk
from a UNIX/Linux/Mac OS X machine, or from a Windows machine with Cygwin.
Alternatively, free clients for Windows implementing SSH shell access 
and file transfer respectively can be downloaded from:
[http://www.chiark.greenend.org.uk/~sgtatham/putty/](http://www.chiark.greenend.org.uk/~sgtatham/putty/)
[http://winscp.net/](http://winscp.net/)
On connecting for the first time you will be asked to accept the HPCS public
key. The correct fingerprint of this key will be reported as one of the
following values:
RSA
MD5:0b:ef:59:90:fb:13:4a:c9:56:82:7b:cd:4b:2b:e1:3b
SHA256:sSkVfzpwjwiFvxLcdPoDpN8IsN3kt0ZSywhDhPKZPAg
ECDSA
MD5:34:9b:f2:d2:c6:b3:5c:63:99:b7:27:da:5b:c8:16:fe
SHA256:HsiY1Oe0M8tS6JwR76PeQQA/VB7r8675BzG5OYQ4h34
ED25519
MD5:64:7c:7c:ff:05:9d:0e:dc:06:fe:f1:c2:10:37:7a:85
SHA256:wq9ljBfPa7lXXpQq+rk5JTBXLJO/kXjOc5A7rp4ENzA 
either:
If you see a different fingerprint at this stage, please CANCEL and contact
support@hpc.cam.ac.uk as soon as possible.
VNC connections tunnelled over SSH are another possible method of accessing
the HPCS - for more information please see:
[http://www.hpc.cam.ac.uk/using-clusters/remote-desktops-and-3d/darwin-turbovnc](http://www.hpc.cam.ac.uk/using-clusters/remote-desktops-and-3d/darwin-turbovnc)
Getting started
—
To get started, please review
[http://www.hpc.cam.ac.uk/using-clusters/quick-start](http://www.hpc.cam.ac.uk/using-clusters/quick-start)
If you choose to change the password, please be sure to change it to something
of comparable complexity. Protect all passwords as you would credit card
information.
Planned maintenance
—
There is a regular planned maintenance slot on Tuesdays. This is not always
taken up but if it is will be advertised in advance. Every reasonable effort
will be made to maintain uninterrupted service otherwise, but external events
such as power or cooling failures do sometimes occur.
Directory permissions
—
By default your home and scratch directories on the HPCS have no permissions
(i.e. no read, write or search) for other users. Your primary group contains
your account only. If you need to relax these permissions, e.g. to allow a
specific colleague access, we recommend you seek advice from the support help
desk unless you are a UNIX expert before attempting to modify the directory
permissions.
Email
—
The system requires the ability to send you job-related emails. Email sent to
your account on the HPCS from the HPCS will be forwarded transparently to the
email address provided on the application form. Users may also send email to
other users in the same way whilst on the HPCS, however the true destination
address for messages sent to you is not visible to another user, unless you
direct email to that user (in which case your message is necessarily given a
valid return address). The HPCS does not accept or process email originating
externally.
The support team also uses email to advise the user base of impending events
such as planned maintenance periods, or emergencies affecting service. This is
done using the University list server and by default all users are added to
the list hpc-user@lists.cam.ac.uk. This is a moderated list and although you
may opt out of this if you wish, we would prefer you did not.
X windows
—
Under UNIX/Linux/Mac OS X, X windows applications may display correctly to
your local machine right away, otherwise please try ssh -Y. These remarks also
apply to Cygwin under Windows (start the X server with startx first). Under no
circumstances should it be necessary to expose your local machine to evildoers
(and then every other machine to which you connect) by using the command xhost
(please contact us if you have display problems).
Free software providing SSH and X windows functionality for Windows can be 
downloaded from:
[http://www.cygwin.org/cygwin/](http://www.cygwin.org/cygwin/)
Other
—
Please note that you are enabled as a SL3 user in the NIK-ZAINAL-SL3/-CLINCLOUD
project.
For news, current system status and documentation please visit
[http://www.hpc.cam.ac.uk/](http://www.hpc.cam.ac.uk/)
In particular, please see
[http://www.hpc.cam.ac.uk/policies](http://www.hpc.cam.ac.uk/policies)
which describes our usage policies, and the FAQ at
[http://www.hpc.cam.ac.uk/getting-help/faq](http://www.hpc.cam.ac.uk/getting-help/faq)
Please let us know at support@hpc.cam.ac.uk if you experience any problems.
Best regards,
Simon Flood
– 
The Wellcome Sanger Institute is operated by Genome Research 
Limited, a charity registered in England with number 1021457 and a 
company registered in England with number 2742969, whose registered 
office is 215 Euston Road, London, NW1 2BE.

[TICKET_SUMMARY] Password issue with the CSD3 cluster

[INITIAL_DESCRIPTION]
To whom it may concern,  
My name is Christopher Moore and I'm a PGR at Bangor University. On Mon, 1 Nov 2021 at 12:48, <[support@hpc.cam.ac.uk](mailto:support@hpc.cam.ac.uk)> wrote:
> Welcome to the Cambridge Service for Data Driven Discovery (CSD3)
> facility. CSD3 is comprised of three distinct clusters:
> Peta4-Skylake - Intel Skylake and Omni-Path CPU cluster
> Peta4-KNL     - Knights Landing and Omni-Path many-core cluster
> Wilkes2-GPU   - NVIDIA P100 and Mellanox EDR IB GPU cluster.
> For more information on the service please see:
> [docs.hpc.cam.ac.uk](http://docs.hpc.cam.ac.uk) 
> Connecting
> ==========
> Firstly, if you are an external user, please email a RSA SSH public
> key to [support@hpc.cam.ac.uk](mailto:support@hpc.cam.ac.uk) (please use at least 2048 bits and don't
> forget to set a passphrase). The id of the user account is as given
> on the Subject line to this email.
> Internal Cambridge users, please use your Cambridge CRSid and
> UIS password.
> All users should login via SSH to any of the addresses below:
> [login-cpu.hpc.cam.ac.uk](http://login-cpu.hpc.cam.ac.uk)
> [login-knl.hpc.cam.ac.uk](http://login-knl.hpc.cam.ac.uk)
> [login-gpu.hpc.cam.ac.uk](http://login-gpu.hpc.cam.ac.uk)
> login-cpu and login-knl will both take you to one of a set of
> OPA-enabled login nodes; these nodes are best suited to development
> work for the Peta4 clusters (Skylake and KNL). login-gpu will take you
> to a different set of Mellanox OFED and CUDA-enabled login nodes best
> suited for development work for Wilkes2-GPU.
> We are allowing unrestricted SSH access to the CSD3 login nodes, but
> have protected them with fail2ban. This means that after repeated
> failed login attempts from the same origin, that origin will be
> blocked from further access for 20 minutes.
> The SSH host keys have the following fingerprints (which of these
> hashes you see will depend on your client):
> RSA
>     MD5:fd:5c:6b:7d:49:95:2f:da:7f:5c:50:9a:bb:ef:3f:24
>     SHA256:2rl+MXd9rsrDzFZwEItmhhiHTlLTIqN0d3TSGLTgjTI
> ED25519
>     MD5:eb:e3:a1:f0:64:68:cf:9c:63:da:84:db:2e:ee:15:83
>     SHA256:nFVSXK+VRGCaUupQEdhXzO6kp01m2fzzmbgPr0sc2so
> As well as SSH login, connection is possible via x2go and VNC (the
> latter needs to be tunnelled over SSH).
> Storage
> =======
> Each user has a hard quota of 40GiB on their home directory. This is
> provided by ZFS over NFS and hourly/daily/weekly snapshots are made
> automatically which can be searched by the user. E.g. if you delete a
> file by accident look in /home/.zfs/snapshot and browse the best
> snapshot for the file (which can be copied back to your home
> directory).
> The working/scratch storage has been implemented as part of the
> Cambridge Research Data Storage (RDS) service. Please look under
> ~/rds/ for your project's new "work" directory (it will have the name
> hpc-work). There is currently a 1TB per user quota enforced on this
> directory. Please note that it is already possible to buy additional,
> larger RDS a

operating system updates, antivirus and anti-spyware software.
We will need to explicitly allow external IP addresses access through our
firewall. Please email us the appropriate originating IP address for your
local network if you have not already done so.
You should be able to login to one of the login nodes of the cluster
simply by doing:
ssh hpckoh1@login.hpc.cam.ac.uk
from a UNIX/Linux/Mac OS X machine, or from a Windows machine with Cygwin.
Alternatively, free clients for Windows implementing SSH shell access 
and file transfer respectively can be downloaded from:
[http://www.chiark.greenend.org.uk/~sgtatham/putty/](http://www.chiark.greenend.org.uk/~sgtatham/putty/)
[http://winscp.net/](http://winscp.net/)
On connecting for the first time you will be asked to accept the HPCS public
key. The correct fingerprint of this key will be reported as one of the
following values:
RSA
MD5:0b:ef:59:90:fb:13:4a:c9:56:82:7b:cd:4b:2b:e1:3b
SHA256:sSkVfzpwjwiFvxLcdPoDpN8IsN3kt0ZSywhDhPKZPAg
ECDSA
MD5:34:9b:f2:d2:c6:b3:5c:63:99:b7:27:da:5b:c8:16:fe
SHA256:HsiY1Oe0M8tS6JwR76PeQQA/VB7r8675BzG5OYQ4h34
ED25519
MD5:64:7c:7c:ff:05:9d:0e:dc:06:fe:f1:c2:10:37:7a:85
SHA256:wq9ljBfPa7lXXpQq+rk5JTBXLJO/kXjOc5A7rp4ENzA 
either:
If you see a different fingerprint at this stage, please CANCEL and contact
support@hpc.cam.ac.uk as soon as possible.
VNC connections tunnelled over SSH are another possible method of accessing
the HPCS - for more information please see:
[http://www.hpc.cam.ac.uk/using-clusters/remote-desktops-and-3d/darwin-turbovnc](http://www.hpc.cam.ac.uk/using-clusters/remote-desktops-and-3d/darwin-turbovnc)
Getting started
—
To get started, please review
[http://www.hpc.cam.ac.uk/using-clusters/quick-start](http://www.hpc.cam.ac.uk/using-clusters/quick-start)
If you choose to change the password, please be sure to change it to something
of comparable complexity. Protect all passwords as you would credit card
information.
Planned maintenance
—
There is a regular planned maintenance slot on Tuesdays. This is not always
taken up but if it is will be advertised in advance. Every reasonable effort
will be made to maintain uninterrupted service otherwise, but external events
such as power or cooling failures do sometimes occur.
Directory permissions
—
By default your home and scratch directories on the HPCS have no permissions
(i.e. no read, write or search) for other users. Your primary group contains
your account only. If you need to relax these permissions, e.g. to allow a
specific colleague access, we recommend you seek advice from the support help
desk unless you are a UNIX expert before attempting to modify the directory
permissions.
Email
—
The system requires the ability to send you job-related emails. Email sent to
your account on the HPCS from the HPCS will be forwarded transparently to the
email address provided on the application form. Users may als

benchmark_annotation.summary: SRCP Epi platform - storage query

metrics.reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: SRCP / Storage Architecture / RFS
Technical Action Required: No (clarification only)
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Low

FOCUS OF RESPONSE
User’s second query:

“My understanding is that somehow storage for SRCP is provisioned on RFS. Is this RFS dedicated hardware that is isolated for use by a particular platform? Or does it work differently? In which case, is there the option to have dedicated RCS hardware, which will be cheaper?”

QUICK ASSESSMENT
SRCP storage is provisioned using RFS-backed storage that is logically and operationally isolated per platform/project. It is not pooled with general-purpose RDS/RCS environments, and users cannot directly mount external RCS storage within SRCP. There is no model for attaching separate “dedicated RCS hardware” to SRCP as a cheaper alternative.

ACTION

1) Clarify storage model
   - SRCP storage is provisioned as isolated RFS shares associated specifically with SRCP projects.
   - These shares are separated from other storage systems and not user-configurable.
   - The isolation is by design due to SRCP security requirements.

2) Clarify hardware/cost question
   - SRCP storage is not dynamically attached from arbitrary RCS pools.
   - There is no option to provision separate “dedicated RCS hardware” for SRCP at a reduced cost.
   - SRCP environments have a defined architecture and approved storage pathways only.

3) If cost optimisation is the underlying concern
   - Suggest reviewing retention strategy (e.g., archive/export workflow outside SRCP if appropriate).
   - Explain that external storage (e.g., RCS) would require controlled export and re-import, not live mounting.

4) Do NOT:
   - Suggest bypassing SRCP isolation.
   - Imply that SRCP storage can be merged with other RCS/RDS resources.
   - Promise alternative hardware provisioning unless formally approved.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Subject: Re: SRCP storage architecture

Dear Tom,

SRCP storage is provisioned using RFS shares that are logically and operationally isolated for SRCP projects. These are separate from other RCS/RDS storage systems and are not directly accessible or mountable from outside the platform.

It is not possible to attach separate “dedicated RCS hardware” to SRCP as a lower-cost alternative. The SRCP architecture and its storage pathways are fixed by design due to the security requirements of the platform.

If you are looking to reduce storage costs, one option would be to export data from SRCP and archive it to a nominated RCS project outside the platform, but this would not be a live integration.

Please let us know if you would like to discuss possible workflows for archiving data.

Best wishes,  
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Maintain SRCP security model integrity.
- Do not suggest storage sharing across security domains.
- Avoid speculative statements about hardware provisioning beyond documented architecture.
metrics.response:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Storage / SRCP [5]
Technical Action Required: Yes [5]
Portal Action Required: Yes (Self Service) [2]

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The user is inquiring about archiving old projects on the platform and wants to know if RFS is the only storage option available or if RCS is available from within the SRCP [5]. 

ACTION
To archive old projects, the user can consider transferring data to an RCS store [3]. The user can purchase an RCS license using the storage portal [2] and then transfer the data from RFS to RCS. Alternatively, the user can use the SRCP to store data, but the availability of storage space needs to be confirmed [5].

QUESTIONS TO ASK (ONLY IF NEEDED)
- What is the estimated size of the data to be archived?
- What is the desired duration for storing the archived data?

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello,
To archive your old projects, you can consider transferring data to an RCS store. You can purchase an RCS license using the storage portal and then transfer the data from RFS to RCS. Alternatively, you can use the SRCP to store data, but please note that the availability of storage space needs to be confirmed. 
Please let us know if you need further assistance.
Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- The user should ensure that they have the necessary permissions and access rights to transfer data between storage systems [1].
- The user should confirm the availability of storage space before transferring data to the SRCP [5].

REFERENCE KEY
[1] : HPCSSUP-61311
[2] : HPCSSUP-66096
[3] : HPCSSUP-44043
[5] : HPCSSUP-82321

﻿On 08/04/2022, 16:09, "Dominic Friend" <support@hpc.cam.ac.uk> wrote:
—-—-—-—
Reply above this line.
Dominic Friend commented:
Hi Sarah,
This is definitely something we can assist with.
We can do a capacity analysis of the directories that you have listed so that we can quote you for a new RCS space. Once that is paid for and created we can facilitate the movement of the data on our end.
Once the copy is complete we can delete the copied data from RFS to allow for the shrinkage. At this point we'll know the remaining usage and we'll round it up to the nearest TB and +1TB as you request. And this is the number we'll shrink the RFS license to in the portal, so then you can renew for the correct cost using the portal.
If things are getting a little tight for time, we can invoke the shrink of RFS in the portal earlier, and delay reflecting the changes on the file-system until all the data is in the correct locations. The portal is mainly for book keeping and doesn't directly interact with file system, so we can intervene in cases like this.
Regarding your RCS lifecycle question can you clarify what you mean by lifecycle? Are you referring to our plans for the lifetime of RCS as a service?
Best,
Dominic

[CONVERSATION]
<MESSAGE id=0001 role=HELPDESK_ASSIGNEE>
Hi Sarah,
This is definitely something we can assist with.
We can do a capacity analysis of the directories that you have listed so that we can quote you for a new RCS space. Once that is paid for and created we can facilitate the movement of the data on our end.
Once the copy is complete we can delete the copied data from RFS to allow for the shrinkage. At this point we'll know the remaining usage and we'll round it up to the nearest TB and +1TB as you request. And this is the number we'll shrink the RFS license to in the portal, so then you can renew for the correct cost using the portal.
If things are getting a little tight for time, we can invoke the shrink of RFS in the portal earlier, and delay reflecting the changes on the file-system until all the data is in the correct locations. The portal is mainly for book keeping and doesn't directly interact with file system, so we can intervene in cases like this.
Regarding your RCS lifecycle question can you clarify what you mean by lifecycle? Are you referring to our plans for the lifetime of RCS as a service?
Best,
Dominic

benchmark_annotation.summary: Adding new user to the lab RFS/RDS space

metrics.reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Storage / RDS & RFS / Provisioning
Technical Action Required: No (user action pending)
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Low–Medium (access pending)

QUICK ASSESSMENT
A new user (vj292) has been added to both an RDS and RFS-NFS project via the self-service portal but remains in “Provisioning” state. The most common cause of this status is that the user has not yet accepted the storage service Terms & Conditions in the UIS Self Service portal. Until this step is completed, the provisioning workflow cannot proceed and access will not be activated.

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)

1) Confirm provisioning state (internal)
   - Verify that the user appears as “Provisioning” on the relevant RDS and RFS projects.
   - Check whether Terms & Conditions acceptance is outstanding.

2) Instruct user to accept Terms & Conditions
   Ask the new member (vj292) to:
   - Visit: https://selfservice.uis.cam.ac.uk/account/
   - Click on each relevant storage project:
       • rds-djh1002-hodson-rds
       • rfs-djh1002-hodson-rfs-nfs
   - Accept the storage Terms & Conditions for each project.

3) Explain workflow
   - Once Terms & Conditions are accepted, a provisioning request is automatically generated.
   - HPC Support will then activate access.
   - All relevant parties will receive automatic email confirmation once provisioning completes.

4) Do NOT:
   - Manually override provisioning before Terms & Conditions are accepted.
   - Escalate unless provisioning remains stalled after acceptance.

IF PROVISIONING DOES NOT COMPLETE AFTER ACCEPTANCE

- Confirm acceptance timestamp in portal.
- Check whether an automatic provisioning ticket was generated.
- Escalate to storage administration only if backend provisioning fails after acceptance.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Subject: Re: Provisioning status for new lab member

Dear Joanna,

Thank you for your message.

reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Storage / RDS & RFS / Provisioning
Technical Action Required: No (user action pending)
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Low–Medium (access pending)

QUICK ASSESSMENT
A new user (vj292) has been added to both an RDS and RFS-NFS project via the self-service portal but remains in “Provisioning” state. The most common cause of this status is that the user has not yet accepted the storage service Terms & Conditions in the UIS Self Service portal. Until this step is completed, the provisioning workflow cannot proceed and access will not be activated.

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)

1) Confirm provisioning state (internal)
   - Verify that the user appears as “Provisioning” on the relevant RDS and RFS projects.
   - Check whether Terms & Conditions acceptance is outstanding.

2) Instruct user to accept Terms & Conditions
   Ask the new member (vj292) to:
   - Visit: https://selfservice.uis.cam.ac.uk/account/
   - Click on each relevant storage project:
       • rds-djh1002-hodson-rds
       • rfs-djh1002-hodson-rfs-nfs
   - Accept the storage Terms & Conditions for each project.

3) Explain workflow
   - Once Terms & Conditions are accepted, a provisioning request is automatically generated.
   - HPC Support will then activate access.
   - All relevant parties will receive automatic email confirmation once provisioning completes.

4) Do NOT:
   - Manually override provisioning before Terms & Conditions are accepted.
   - Escalate unless provisioning remains stalled after acceptance.

IF PROVISIONING DOES NOT COMPLETE AFTER ACCEPTANCE

- Confirm acceptance timestamp in portal.
- Check whether an automatic provisioning ticket was generated.
- Escalate to storage administration only if backend provisioning fails after acceptance.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Subject: Re: Provisioning status for new lab member

Dear Joanna,

Thank you for your message.

Victor’s access is currently showing as “Provisioning” because he has not yet accepted the storage service Terms & Conditions.

Could he please visit:

  https://selfservice.uis.cam.ac.uk/account/

and click on the relevant project links to accept the Terms & Conditions for each storage project?

Once this is completed, a provisioning request will automatically be generated and we will activate his access. All parties will receive confirmation by email once this has been processed.

Best wishes,  
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Do not bypass the mandatory Terms & Conditions acceptance process.
- Do not grant access before provisioning workflow completes.
- Ensure both RDS and RFS projects are covered if both were requested.

Subject: Re: RFS group
Hi Wanne,
Thanks for discussing with the group, I hope it went down ok over all
- Storage Limits -> There is a set storage limit for the entire RFS (currently 1TB). This can very easily be extended as and when needed. So there is no specific user storage cap.
- RFS is an active connection, so the only way to connect to it is to map it as a network drive/location and access the files directly. Having said that, users could utilise software like RSync to sync between the Mapped Drive and a local folder on their PC, but they would need to be careful only to sync their files and not the entire RFS.
- You will need to send the invites through the Self-Service portal. If you like, I can do this for you. It might be good practice to do it yourselves within the group though. Entirely up to you.
Hope this all helps,
Kind regards
Shaun Cooke (he/him)

University of Cambridge

benchmark_annotation.summary: Getting write permission to rds project

metrics.reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Storage / RDS Permissions / Project Roles
Technical Action Required: No (permission governance guidance)
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Medium (write access blocked)

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User requests read/write access to an RDS project and assumes only one Data Manager can exist. On RDS projects, multiple Data Managers are allowed. If the user is currently a Data User, write capability should be granted by project governance (Data Owner/Data Manager) either by role reassignment or by applying permissions according to RDS permissions guidance.

ACTION

1) Clarify role model
   - Data Owners can appoint multiple Data Managers.
   - Data Users cannot self-assign elevated permissions.

2) Direct user to project governance path
   - Ask user to contact the Data Owner or existing Data Manager for:
     a) role change to Data Manager, or
     b) permission grant aligned with RDS ACL guidance.

3) Provide documentation link
   https://docs.hpc.cam.ac.uk/storage/rds/permissions.html

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Hello Emilia,

Data Owners can add as many Data Managers as required on an RDS project.

If you are currently a Data User, you will need to ask the Data Owner or a Data Manager to either:
1) reassign your role to Data Manager, or  
2) grant the required permissions in line with the RDS permissions guidance:
https://docs.hpc.cam.ac.uk/storage/rds/permissions.html

Best wishes,  
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Do not change project roles/permissions without Data Owner or Data Manager authorisation.
- Do not imply Helpdesk can bypass project governance for access control.
metrics.response:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Storage / Permissions
Technical Action Required: Yes
Escalation Required: Possibly
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
By default, RDS projects are set to allow only managers group full read/write permission and users and visitors group get read-only access [3][6]. To get write access, the user needs to be added to the project's managers group on the portal [3] or have the permissions changed by a manager [6].

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
To resolve the issue, we can add the user to the project's managers group or change the permissions to allow the users group to have read/write access. We can use the `setfacl` command to grant read/write permission to all users to existing and future files [8]. Alternatively, we can use standard tools like `chown`/`chmod` to make the users group the group owner of the directory and set the group to have read/write permissions [8].

QUESTIONS TO ASK (ONLY IF NEEDED)
- Can you please confirm the project ID and the user's role in the project?
- Are there any specific subdirectories where you need write access?

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello,
To resolve the issue, we can add you to the project's managers group or change the permissions to allow the users group to have read/write access. Please let us know which option you prefer. If you need help with changing the permissions, we can assist you with that.
Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- By default, RDS projects are set to allow only managers group full read/write permission and users and visitors group get read-only access [3][6].
- Only data managers or the overall owner of the project can request changes to the permissions [8].

REFERENCE KEY
[3] : HPCSSUP-34408
[6] : HPCSSUP-51660
[8] : HPCSSUP-45623

   - Data Users cannot self-assign elevated permissions.

2) Direct user to project governance path
   - Ask user to contact the Data Owner or existing Data Manager for:
     a) role change to Data Manager, or
     b) permission grant aligned with RDS ACL guidance.

3) Provide documentation link
   https://docs.hpc.cam.ac.uk/storage/rds/permissions.html

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Hello Emilia,

Data Owners can add as many Data Managers as required on an RDS project.

If you are currently a Data User, you will need to ask the Data Owner or a Data Manager to either:
1) reassign your role to Data Manager, or  
2) grant the required permissions in line with the RDS permissions guidance:
https://docs.hpc.cam.ac.uk/storage/rds/permissions.html

Best wishes,  
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Do not change project roles/permissions without Data Owner or Data Manager authorisation.
- Do not imply Helpdesk can bypass project governance for access control.
response:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Storage / Permissions
Technical Action Required: Yes
Escalation Required: Possibly
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
By default, RDS projects are set to allow only managers group full read/write permission and users and visitors group get read-only access [3][6]. To get write access, the user needs to be added to the project's managers group on the portal [3] or have the permissions changed by a manager [6].

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
To resolve the issue, we can add the user to the project's managers group or change the permissions to allow the users group to have read/write access. We can use the `setfacl` command to grant read/write permission to all users to existing and future files [8]. Alternatively, we can use standard tools like `chown`/`chmod` to make the users group the group owner of the directory and set the group to have read/write permissions [8].

QUESTIONS TO ASK (ONLY IF NEEDED)
- Can you please confirm the project ID and the user's role in the project?
- Are there any specific subdirectories where you need write access?

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello,
To resolve the issue, we can add you to the project's managers group or change the permissions to allow the users group to have read/write access. Please let us know which option you prefer. If you need help with changing the permissions, we can assist you with that.
Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- By default, RDS projects are set to allow only managers group full read/write permission and users and visitors group get read-only access [3][6].
- Only data managers or the overall owner of the project can request changes to the permissions [8].

REFERENCE KEY
[3] : HPCSSUP-34408
[6] : HPCSSUP-51660
[8] : HPCSSUP-45623

Hi Hugo,
This is a default with new projects now. Apart from storage portal where managers add/remove users they also get to manage project directories to more granular level.  If you are the manager and want to open write access to user (or visitors) group then you can achieve this with few acl commands i.e.
setfacl -Rdm g:<name-of-group>:rwx /<path_to_project>/<project_folder> 
This also works for single users with small change:
setfacl -Rdm u:<name-of-group>:rwx /<path_to_project>/<project_folder> 
To list who has access 
getfacl /<path_to_project>/<project_folder> 
 
Regards,
Greg
RCS Team

Hi Hugo,
This is a default with new projects now. Apart from storage portal where managers add/remove users they also get to manage project directories to more granular level.  If you are the manager and want to open write access to user (or visitors) group then you can achieve this with few acl commands i.e.
setfacl -Rdm g:<name-of-group>:rwx /<path_to_project>/<project_folder> 
This also works for single users with small change:
setfacl -Rdm u:<name-of-group>:rwx /<path_to_project>/<project_folder> 
To list who has access 
getfacl /<path_to_project>/<project_folder> 
 
Regards,
Greg
RCS Team
